chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. C.P.No.D- 360 of 2019 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A 2028/19 2. For orders on office objection 3. For orders on M.A 2029/19 4. For hearing of main case 27.02.2019 Mr. Saeed Ahmed Mirjat, Advocate for petitioners. -.-.-. 1. Granted. 2to6. Petitioners claim to be Managers and Booking Officers of the booking offices within their respective areas located on the route between Larkana to Karachi and booking passengers for various coaches plying between Larkana to Hyderabad via Dadu-Sehwan. Their grievance is that they are being harassed by the respondents No.2 to 5 to shift the stop of the vehicles to Makhdoom Bilawal from Dadu City. We have asked the learned Counsel to satisfy about maintainability of this petition as the adequate remedy is available to the petitioners before the government officials concerned, he submits that it is the fundamental right of the petitioners to approach this court. As per this file, petitioners have already filed an application, available at Page-13 of the file, which is addressed to the Deputy Commissioner, Dadu, SSP Dadu and District Transport Authority, Dadu for redressal of their grievance. Instead of pursuing the said application they have filed this petition. No document has been placed on record to show that the said application has been decided by the authorities to warrant interference by this court. This being the position, the petition is not maintainable and is dismissed in limine alongwith applications listed at serial No.3. The petitioner may however, avail the remedy provided to him under the law.

This being the position, the petition is not maintainable and is dismissed in limine alongwith applications listed at serial No.3. The petitioner may however, avail the remedy provided to him under the law. JUDGE JUDGE Ali Haider
